Prompt
U
Oracle Events
Search emails for Raj Greenfield's invoice complaint using search_emails (query: "Raj Greenfield" or "Sunbelt" or "INV-2026-0398" or "invoice"). Find three emails from raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com: (a) initial email to emeka.diallo@moveops.com (April 14) asking about a $2,400 amount under a Palmetto Community Foundation header on what should have been a Sunbelt invoice, asking if this is a new rate card, wrong attachment, or nonprofit discount program; (b) follow-up email to emeka.diallo@moveops.com (April 15) reiterating that the PDF still shows Palmetto at $2,400 for what he understood were two Sunbelt relocations and requesting same-day clarification; and (c) escalation email to david.chen@moveops.com (April 15) noting he hasn't gotten a clear answer from Emeka and asking David to confirm whether the $2,400 / Palmetto header is a wrong attachment or a pricing discrepancy. All three emails are unread, indicating neither Emeka nor David had responded at the time of discovery.
Search emails for Palmetto-related communications using search_emails (query: "Palmetto" or "Carmen Delgado-Reyes" or "INV-2026-0399" or "audit"). Find two emails from carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org: (a) initial email (April 14) flagging that the PDF from Hana's April 10 email shows $4,800 under a Sunbelt Venture Partners header, roughly double what Palmetto expected, raising this as an audit risk ahead of Palmetto's May 15 audit deadline, requesting confirmation of the correct amount, a written explanation, and a corrected invoice; and (b) follow-up email (April 15) pressing that she cannot route the invoice for payment or keep it in the audit file, and needs the corrected invoice PDF and brief written explanation today. Both emails are unread.
Search emails for Victoria Chen-Nakamura using search_emails (query: "Victoria" or "portfolio pricing" or "Sunbelt"). Find email from victoria.chen-nakamura@sunbeltvp.com to david.chen@moveops.com (CC: raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com) escalating the pricing concern to a portfolio-wide commercial question, she asks whether MoveOps can offer a tiered rate for Sunbelt-backed companies after seeing the nonprofit pricing level. This expands the risk from a simple billing correction to a commercial pricing exposure.
Search emails for Hana Kim's April invoice batch to trace the swap using search_emails (query: "Hana" or "April invoice" or "batch"). Find two outgoing emails from hana.kim@moveops.com sent on April 10: (a) to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com with subject "MoveOps April invoice INV-2026-0398" stating $4,800 total, but the attached PDF was Palmetto's nonprofit-rate document; (b) to carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org with subject "MoveOps April invoice INV-2026-0399" stating $2,400 total, but the attached PDF was Sunbelt's commercial-rate document. The QuickBooks records were correct; only the PDF attachments were swapped.
Search QuickBooks for Sunbelt invoice using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: matching "Sunbelt" or "INV-2026-0398"). Find invoice Id "398", DocNumber INV-2026-0398, TotalAmt $4,800, CustomerRef "Sunbelt Venture Partners" (cust_sunbelt), TxnDate 2026-04-10, DueDate 2026-05-10. Confirms the QuickBooks record is correct at $4,800 for standard commercial rate.
Search QuickBooks for Palmetto invoice using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: matching "Palmetto" or "INV-2026-0399"). Find invoice Id "399", DocNumber INV-2026-0399, TotalAmt $2,400, CustomerRef "Palmetto Community Foundation" (cust_palmetto), TxnDate 2026-04-10, DueDate 2026-05-10. Confirms the QuickBooks record is correct at $2,400 for nonprofit rate.
Search Slack for Sunbelt/Palmetto incident discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice swap" or "attachment"). Find messages in multiple channels: (a) Marcus Thorne in #finance identifying the dual-risk framing, pricing exposure for Sunbelt and nonprofit compliance/audit-trail risk for Palmetto, confirming QuickBooks is correct but emailed PDFs were crossed; (b) David Chen in #customer-engagement asking Emeka about Raj's email and the nonprofit pricing question; (c) Emeka Diallo's reconstruction message in #customer-engagement acknowledging the swap and confirming he sent corrected communications; (d) Hana Kim's message in #finance noting the April batch send. Read relevant thread replies using conversations_replies.
Search Slack for Emeka's overload flag using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "overload" or "underwater" or "five active", filter_in_channel: "C006"). Find Emeka Diallo's April 5 message flagging he is carrying five active relocations across four clients (Sunbelt, Palmetto, Axiom, Mosaic) and requesting help with coverage. Find Chloe Vance's reply acknowledging the concern but not reassigning. This establishes a contributing cause: Emeka's capacity overload led to delayed client responses after the invoice swap.
Search CRM for Sunbelt Venture Partners using crm_search_companies (name: "Sunbelt"). Find two company records: company_sunbelt (Miami, FL, FinTech, 25 employees) and crm_company_541459a0c22f (Austin, TX, Venture Capital, 42 employees). Search for associated deals using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Sunbelt"). Find deal_sunbelt_won ($14,000, closedWon), deal_sunbelt_repeat ($7,000, closedWon), and crm_deal_5464b8677e6a ($4,800, April 2026 billing, stage: contractsent).
Search CRM for Palmetto Community Foundation using crm_search_companies (name: "Palmetto"). Find crm_company_69f1ae00a4b9 (Charleston, SC, Nonprofit, 28 employees). Search for associated deals using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Palmetto" or company_id: "crm_company_69f1ae00a4b9"). Find deal_palmetto_won ($3,200, closedWon) and crm_deal_7ec892bc6085 ($2,400, April 2026 billing, stage: contractsent).
Search Linear for the invoice attachment safety ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "invoice" or "attachment" or "safety" or "mismatch"). Find linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2: "Invoice email template safety checks, prevent attachment mismatches", team: Engineering, state: backlog, priority: P2, assignee: Lena Bjorkstrom, created 2026-03-18, due 2026-04-18 (overdue). Description references the Terraform pricing leak as the originating incident. The ticket was deprioritized on April 6 for DOT compliance work and never reassigned.
Search Linear for the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident reconstruction ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "reconstruct"). Find linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130: "Reconstruct Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice attachment exposure and issue corrected documentation", priority: P1, assignee: Marcus Thorne, created 2026-04-15. Read comments using linear_list_comments to find Marcus's framing (second pricing-exposure incident), Samira's engineering admission (known control gap left open), and David's customer-engagement view comparing to Terraform.
Search emails for Samira Tariq's engineering assessment using search_emails (query: "Samira" or "attachment" or "safeguard" or "control gap"). Find Samira's email to Marcus and David acknowledging that engineering identified this exact class of risk after the Terraform pricing exposure and opened a ticket, but Lena deprioritized it on April 6 for DOT compliance work and Samira allowed it to remain in backlog without an assignee or temporary operational control.
Look up Raj Greenfield's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Raj Greenfield" or "Greenfield"). Find raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com. Also look up Victoria Chen-Nakamura if needed for context.
Look up Palmetto's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Carmen Delgado-Reyes" or "Palmetto"). Find carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org, Executive Director at Palmetto Community Foundation.
Log the incident on the Sunbelt CRM record using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: incident summary, company_ids: ["company_sunbelt"] or ["crm_company_541459a0c22f"]). Engagement body should document: invoice INV-2026-0398 was correctly generated at $4,800 in QuickBooks, but the emailed PDF attachment was swapped with Palmetto's nonprofit-rate document showing $2,400. Raj Greenfield and Victoria Chen-Nakamura escalated asking about nonprofit pricing availability. David Chen sent a clarification email confirming Sunbelt's correct rate. Victoria escalated to a portfolio pricing inquiry.
Log the incident on the Palmetto CRM record using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: incident summary, company_ids: ["crm_company_69f1ae00a4b9"]). Engagement body should document: invoice INV-2026-0399 was correctly generated at $2,400 in QuickBooks, but the emailed PDF attachment was swapped with Sunbelt's commercial-rate document showing $4,800. Carmen Delgado-Reyes flagged this as an audit risk with a May 15 deadline. A corrected written explanation was sent.
Send email to Raj Greenfield using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com"]). Email should: (a) acknowledge the invoice confusion, (b) explain that the PDF he received was a misdirected attachment from a different client account sent in error during a batch invoice process, (c) confirm that Sunbelt's correct April invoice is INV-2026-0398 for $4,800 at Sunbelt's standard commercial rate, (d) clarify that MoveOps does not have a general nonprofit pricing program available to commercial clients, and (e) offer to send a clean copy of the correct invoice.
Send email to Carmen Delgado-Reyes at Palmetto using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org"]). Email should: (a) acknowledge the incorrect document received, (b) explain that the $4,800 PDF was a misdirected attachment from a different client account, (c) confirm that Palmetto's correct April invoice is INV-2026-0399 for $2,400 at the agreed nonprofit rate, and (d) provide or attach a corrected document suitable for Palmetto's audit records ahead of the May 15 deadline.
Update the engineering invoice safety ticket using linear_update_issue (id: "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2", priority: 1 or 0 to escalate from P2 to P1 or Urgent). Additionally, add a comment using linear_create_comment (issueId: "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2", body: explaining this is the second pricing-exposure incident after the Terraform reply-all and requesting immediate assignment and action). This flags the systemic engineering gap as the prompt requires.
Reply to Victoria Chen-Nakamura's portfolio pricing escalation using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["victoria.chen-nakamura@sunbeltvp.com"], cc: ["raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com"]) or reply_to_email (email_id: Victoria's original email ID, sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", cc: ["raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com"]). Email should: (a) acknowledge Victoria's interest in discussing portfolio-wide pricing for Sunbelt-backed companies, (b) clarify that the pricing she saw on the misdirected document was from a different client account with a custom arrangement and does not represent a generally available rate, (c) confirm that Sunbelt's correct April invoice is INV-2026-0398 for $4,800, and (d) make clear that any broader portfolio pricing discussion needs to happen separately from this billing correction. The prompt explicitly says: "If Sunbelt leadership escalated about portfolio pricing, reply to that from me as well."
Send Hana Kim a note about the batch process issue using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["hana.kim@moveops.com"]). Email should: (a) flag that the April 10 invoice batch had crossed PDF attachments between Sunbelt Venture Partners (INV-2026-0398) and Palmetto Community Foundation (INV-2026-0399), and (b) ask Hana to double-check the rest of her April sends to confirm no other clients in the batch received mismatched documents. The prompt explicitly says: "Send Hana a note flagging the batch process issue and asking her to double-check the rest of her April sends."
Post a summary in the team Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002" for #customer-engagement, or "C001" for #general). Message should summarize the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident for team alignment: what happened (Hana's April 10 batch sent swapped PDF attachments, Sunbelt received Palmetto's $2,400 nonprofit invoice, Palmetto received Sunbelt's $4,800 commercial invoice), what has been done (QuickBooks confirmed correct, corrective emails sent to Raj and Carmen, CRM engagement notes logged on both accounts, engineering safety ticket escalated to Urgent), and what remains open (Victoria's portfolio pricing conversation, Hana's batch verification, engineering safeguard build). The prompt explicitly says: "post a summary in the team channel so everyone is aligned."
Search QuickBooks to verify the rest of the April invoicing batch is clean using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: matching April 2026 invoices). Find additional April invoices across Canopy Health, Vectral Systems, GreenStack Energy, PivotPoint Labs, Mosaic Robotics, BrightLoop Analytics, and NorthWind Technologies. Verify that no other invoices in the batch appear to have crossed or mismatched attachments. The prompt explicitly says: "verify that the rest of the April invoicing batch is clean because I do not want to discover more crossed attachments later."
Check calendar for upcoming meetings with Sunbelt or Palmetto contacts using calendar_search_events (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "Raj Greenfield" or "Carmen" or "Victoria") or calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime: "2026-04-26", end_datetime: "2026-05-10"). Find no upcoming meetings, the calendar is currently empty. The prompt explicitly says: "check if I have any upcoming meetings with Sunbelt or Palmetto contacts so I know what I am walking into."
Search Airtable for Sunbelt and Palmetto relocation records to pull full relocation history using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "Sunbelt Venture Partners") and separately (value: "Palmetto Community Foundation"). Also search Client Accounts table using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Client Accounts", field_name: "Client", value: "Sunbelt"/"Palmetto") to find account-level relationship details including account manager, contract type, and relocation count. The prompt asks to "pull up the full account and relocation history for both clients."

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Search emails for Raj Greenfield's invoice complaint using search_emails (query: "Raj Greenfield" or "Sunbelt" or "INV-2026-0398" or "invoice"). Find three emails from raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com: (a) initial email to emeka.diallo@moveops.com (April 14) asking about a $2,400 amount under a Palmetto Community Foundation header on what should have been a Sunbelt invoice, asking if this is a new rate card, wrong attachment, or nonprofit discount program; (b) follow-up email to emeka.diallo@moveops.com (April 15) reiterating that the PDF still shows Palmetto at $2,400 for what he understood were two Sunbelt relocations and requesting same-day clarification; and (c) escalation email to david.chen@moveops.com (April 15) noting he hasn't gotten a clear answer from Emeka and asking David to confirm whether the $2,400 / Palmetto header is a wrong attachment or a pricing discrepancy. All three emails are unread, indicating neither Emeka nor David had responded at the time of discovery.

Find linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130: "Reconstruct Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice attachment exposure and issue corrected documentation", priority: P1, assignee: Marcus Thorne, created 2026-04-15. Read comments using linear_list_comments to find Marcus's framing (second pricing-exposure incident), Samira's engineering admission (known control gap left open), and David's customer-engagement view comparing to Terraform.
Search emails for Samira Tariq's engineering assessment using search_emails (query: "Samira" or "attachment" or "safeguard" or "control gap"). Find Samira's email to Marcus and David acknowledging that engineering identified this exact class of risk after the Terraform pricing exposure and opened a ticket, but Lena deprioritized it on April 6 for DOT compliance work and Samira allowed it to remain in backlog without an assignee or temporary operational control.
Look up Raj Greenfield's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Raj Greenfield" or "Greenfield"). Find raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com. Also look up Victoria Chen-Nakamura if needed for context.
Look up Palmetto's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Carmen Delgado-Reyes" or "Palmetto"). Find carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org, Executive Director at Palmetto Community Foundation.
Log the incident on the Sunbelt CRM record using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: incident summary, company_ids: ["company_sunbelt"] or ["crm_company_541459a0c22f"]). Engagement body should document: invoice INV-2026-0398 was correctly generated at $4,800 in QuickBooks, but the emailed PDF attachment was swapped with Palmetto's nonprofit-rate document showing $2,400. Raj Greenfield and Victoria Chen-Nakamura escalated asking about nonprofit pricing availability. David Chen sent a clarification email confirming Sunbelt's correct rate. Victoria escalated to a portfolio pricing inquiry.
Log the incident on the Palmetto CRM record using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", body: incident summary, company_ids: ["crm_company_69f1ae00a4b9"]). Engagement body should document: invoice INV-2026-0399 was correctly generated at $2,400 in QuickBooks, but the emailed PDF attachment was swapped with Sunbelt's commercial-rate document showing $4,800. Carmen Delgado-Reyes flagged this as an audit risk with a May 15 deadline. A corrected written explanation was sent.
Send email to Raj Greenfield using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com"]). Email should: (a) acknowledge the invoice confusion, (b) explain that the PDF he received was a misdirected attachment from a different client account sent in error during a batch invoice process, (c) confirm that Sunbelt's correct April invoice is INV-2026-0398 for $4,800 at Sunbelt's standard commercial rate, (d) clarify that MoveOps does not have a general nonprofit pricing program available to commercial clients, and (e) offer to send a clean copy of the correct invoice.
Send email to Carmen Delgado-Reyes at Palmetto using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org"]). Email should: (a) acknowledge the incorrect document received, (b) explain that the $4,800 PDF was a misdirected attachment from a different client account, (c) confirm that Palmetto's correct April invoice is INV-2026-0399 for $2,400 at the agreed nonprofit rate, and (d) provide or attach a corrected document suitable for Palmetto's audit records ahead of the May 15 deadline.
Update the engineering invoice safety ticket using linear_update_issue (id: "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2", priority: 1 or 0 to escalate from P2 to P1 or Urgent). Additionally, add a comment using linear_create_comment (issueId: "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2", body: explaining this is the second pricing-exposure incident after the Terraform reply-all and requesting immediate assignment and action). This flags the systemic engineering gap as the prompt requires.
Reply to Victoria Chen-Nakamura's portfolio pricing escalation using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["victoria.chen-nakamura@sunbeltvp.com"], cc: ["raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com"]) or reply_to_email (email_id: Victoria's original email ID, sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", cc: ["raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com"]). Email should: (a) acknowledge Victoria's interest in discussing portfolio-wide pricing for Sunbelt-backed companies, (b) clarify that the pricing she saw on the misdirected document was from a different client account with a custom arrangement and does not represent a generally available rate, (c) confirm that Sunbelt's correct April invoice is INV-2026-0398 for $4,800, and (d) make clear that any broader portfolio pricing discussion needs to happen separately from this billing correction. The prompt explicitly says: "If Sunbelt leadership escalated about portfolio pricing, reply to that from me as well."
Send Hana Kim a note about the batch process issue using send_email (sender: "david.chen@moveops.com", recipients: ["hana.kim@moveops.com"]). Email should: (a) flag that the April 10 invoice batch had crossed PDF attachments between Sunbelt Venture Partners (INV-2026-0398) and Palmetto Community Foundation (INV-2026-0399), and (b) ask Hana to double-check the rest of her April sends to confirm no other clients in the batch received mismatched documents. The prompt explicitly says: "Send Hana a note flagging the batch process issue and asking her to double-check the rest of her April sends."
Post a summary in the team Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002" for #customer-engagement, or "C001" for #general). Message should summarize the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident for team alignment: what happened (Hana's April 10 batch sent swapped PDF attachments, Sunbelt received Palmetto's $2,400 nonprofit invoice, Palmetto received Sunbelt's $4,800 commercial invoice), what has been done (QuickBooks confirmed correct, corrective emails sent to Raj and Carmen, CRM engagement notes logged on both accounts, engineering safety ticket escalated to Urgent), and what remains open (Victoria's portfolio pricing conversation, Hana's batch verification, engineering safeguard build). The prompt explicitly says: "post a summary in the team channel so everyone is aligned."
Search QuickBooks to verify the rest of the April invoicing batch is clean using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: matching April 2026 invoices). Find additional April invoices across Canopy Health, Vectral Systems, GreenStack Energy, PivotPoint Labs, Mosaic Robotics, BrightLoop Analytics, and NorthWind Technologies. Verify that no other invoices in the batch appear to have crossed or mismatched attachments. The prompt explicitly says: "verify that the rest of the April invoicing batch is clean because I do not want to discover more crossed attachments later."
Check calendar for upcoming meetings with Sunbelt or Palmetto contacts using calendar_search_events (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "Raj Greenfield" or "Carmen" or "Victoria") or calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime: "2026-04-26", end_datetime: "2026-05-10"). Find no upcoming meetings, the calendar is currently empty. The prompt explicitly says: "check if I have any upcoming meetings with Sunbelt or Palmetto contacts so I know what I am walking into."
Search Airtable for Sunbelt and Palmetto relocation records to pull full relocation history using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "Sunbelt Venture Partners") and separately (value: "Palmetto Community Foundation"). Also search Client Accounts table using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Client Accounts", field_name: "Client", value: "Sunbelt"/"Palmetto") to find account-level relationship details including account manager, contract type, and relocation count. The prompt asks to "pull up the full account and relocation history for both clients."

21 The email to Victoria Chen-Nakamura (visible in send_email or reply_to_email parameters) acknowledges her interest in portfolio pricing but clarifies that the discussion needs to happen separately from the billing correction, and that the pricing she saw was from a misdirected attachment belonging to a different client account (or similar).
outcome

✓Run #2The email to Victoria acknowledges her interest ('I understand that seeing a materially different number raises a legitimate commercial question') and explicitly separates it ('it needs to happen on its own terms, separate from this billing correction'). The email also explains the misdirected attachment.
✓Run #3The email to Victoria acknowledges the portfolio pricing question ('I am genuinely interested in the portfolio conversation you described'), clarifies the pricing she saw was a 'misdirected attachment' belonging to 'a different type of organization', and separates it from the billing correction by offering a proper commercial conversation.
✓Run #4The email to Victoria explicitly acknowledges her portfolio-wide pricing interest ('I understand why this raised a commercial conversation, and I do not want to dismiss that interest') and separates it from the billing correction ('it should be evaluated on its own merits and scoped properly, not anchored to a billing mistake'), also clarifying the pricing she saw was a misdirected attachment.
✓Run #5The reply to Victoria explicitly acknowledges 'the broader question you raised about portfolio-wide pricing for Sunbelt-backed companies' as a legitimate conversation, while clarifying the $2,400 was 'a PDF attachment swap during a batch send' and offering to schedule a separate call decoupled from the billing correction.
✓Run #6The email to Victoria acknowledges her portfolio pricing interest ('I hear your broader question about whether there is a portfolio-wide pricing structure...'), explains the pricing was from a misdirected attachment ('The $2,400 invoice Raj forwarded to you belongs to a different MoveOps client'), and clearly separates the discussion ('I would suggest we set up a separate call to explore portfolio pricing once this invoice matter is fully closed out').
